bio.education: University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Son of Casey and Cheryl Dodson
  • U.S. Marine veteran, Purple Heart recipient
  • Competed on the wheelchair track and field team at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign
Biographical Information

Travis Dodson grew up in New Mexico and was a member of his high school golf team. He joined the U.S. Marine Corps after high school, and while serving a tour in Iraq on Valentine’s Day 2007, a grenade struck him, causing him to become a double amputee, losing his left leg at the hip and right leg below the knee.

While a student at the University of Illinois, Dodson became involved in adaptive sports, competing on the Illini wheelchair track and field team with future Team USA teammates Tatyana McFadden and Aaron Pike. All three went on to make the U.S. para Nordic skiing team for the Paralympic Winter Games Sochi 2014. Even though Dodson primarily competed in biathlon at the Sochi Games, he was drawn to the sled hockey team, which was winning gold and their camaraderie, and he took up sled hockey the following year.
Dodson made the sled hockey team for the Paralympic Winter Games PyeongChang 2018 and Paralympic Winter Games Beijing 2022, winning back-to-back gold medals with the squad.

Paralympic Experience
  • 4-time Paralympian; 3-time Paralympic medalist (3 gold)
    • Paralympic Games Milan 2026, gold (Team - Mixed)
    • Paralympic Games Beijing 2022, gold (Team - Mixed)
    • Paralympic Games Pyeongchang 2018, gold (Team - Mixed)
    • Paralympic Games Sochi 2014, 19th (1km Sprint - Sitting - Men), 20th (7.5km Sprint - Sitting - Men), 21th (15km Individual - Sitting - Men), 23th (10km - Sitting - Men)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2025 – gold (Team - Men)
  • Years of participation: Team - Men 2019, 2021, 2023, 2024, 2025
  • Medals: 5 (4 gold, 1 silver)
    • Gold – 2025 (Team - Men); 2023 (Team - Men); 2021 (Team - Men); 2019 (Team - Men)
    • Silver – 2024 (Team - Men)

bio.education: Classical Academy, Escondido California
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Lera was born in Saratov, Russia with a congenital condition called Arthrogryposis of the lower extremities. Lera was adopted and came to the U.S. in 2005. Until she was 14 years-old she relied on leg braces and hand crutches for mobility. At 14 years-old she made the decision to have both of her legs amputated for better mobility and better quality of life.
  • Lera was at a camp for amputees and met Beth Ann Chamberlain, who thought she would be good at the sport. After a year, she attended a ski spec camp, loved it and never looked back
  • Lera is very active. She enjoys many sports including Para Nordic skiing, sled hockey, hand-cycling, adaptive surfing, wheelchair lacrosse and sitting volleyball
  • Her parents are David & Hiromi ‘Fami’ Doederlein and her siblings are Isaac, Keenan & Tori
  • Her full name is Valeria.
  • Her hobbies include adaptive sports, Netflix and popcorn, PS4
  • Her sporting idols are Oksana Masters and Isaac Doederlein (Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu Featherweight, world ranked second).
  • Her favorite thing about the sport is the speed and freedom.
  • Her most memorable moment was when she participated in the 2019 USA/Canada Sled Hockey rivalry series
  • Lera would describe herself as determined, passionate and optimistic
  • In addition to Para Nordic Skiing and Biathlon, Lera has been actively training and competing in sled hockey as a member of USA Hockey Women’s Sled Development Team and San Diego Ducks Sled Hockey team. She has also been training in hand cycling as a member of CAF Women’s Handcycling Team. Lera's attitude is to try every sport at least once.
Paralympic Experience
  • 1-time Paralympian
    • Paralympic Games Beijing 2022, 9th (10km Middle - Sitting - Women, 6km Individual - Sitting - Women), 14th (1km Sprint - Sitting - Women, 7.5km - Sitting - Women)
More

bio.education: Montclair Kimberly Academy (Montclair, N.J.) ‘78 New England College ’82, Biology/Bioengineering
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Doerr grew up sailing on Barnegat Bay in New Jersey, where all of his friends and family were avid sailors. He was forced to crew for his siblings at a young age. After a spinal cord injury in 1992, Doerr was able to reconnect to his past and has stated that sailing has improved his health
  • Son of Alphonsus and Esther Doerr
  • Married to Joann Doerr
  • Has four sisters, Carol, Debra, Mary and Susan, and one brother, Joe
  • His career as a physician in plastic and reconstructive surgery is another one of his passions
  • Coaches Bergen Catholic High School lacrosse
  • He is an assistant professor of orthopedics at Seton Hall University
  • Hobbies include lacrosse, skiing and coaching.
Paralympic Experience
  • 2-time Paralympian; 1-time Paralympic medalist (1 silver)
    • Paralympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, silver (Three-Person Keelboat - Mixed)

bio.fun_fact: Member of U.S. Army World Class Athlete Program
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • The youngest U.S. athlete to compete in biathlon at the Olympic Winter Games Sochi 2014
  • Member of U.S. Army World Class Athlete Program
Biographical Information

Sean Doherty grew up in Center Conway, New Hampshire, and began competing in biathlon at age 12. He became the first teenager and the youngest U.S. athlete to compete in biathlon at the Sochi Games. He has been a member of the U.S. biathlon team at eight world championships and qualified for his fourth U.S. Olympic team for the Olympic Winter Games Milano Cortina 2026.

Doherty joined the U.S. Army in 2018 and is a member of the U.S. Army World Class Athlete Program. Doherty is a carpentry and mason specialist with the Vermont Army National Guard.

Olympic Experience
  • 4-time Olympian
    • Olympic Games Milan 2026, 5th (4 x 7.5km Relay - Men), 65th (10km Sprint - Men), 80th (20km Individual - Men)
    • Olympic Games Beijing 2022, 7th (4 x 6km Relay - Mixed), 13th (4 x 7.5km Relay - Men), 42th (20km Individual - Men), 43th (12.5km Pursuit - Men), 47th (10km Sprint - Men)
    • Olympic Games Pyeongchang 2018, 6th (4 x 7.5km Relay - Men), 44th (20km Individual - Men), 65th (10km Sprint - Men)
    • Olympic Games Sochi 2014, 15th (4 x 7.5km Relay - Men)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2025 – 9th (4 x 7.5km Relay - Men), 41th (10km Sprint - Men), 45th (12.5km Pursuit - Men)
  • Years of participation: 10km Sprint - Men 2015, 2016, 2017, 2019, 2020, 2021, 2023, 2024, 2025; 12.5km Pursuit - Men 2015, 2016, 2017, 2019, 2020, 2024, 2025; 4 x 7.5km Relay - Men 2015, 2016, 2017, 2020, 2021, 2023, 2024, 2025; 15km Mass Start - Men 2019, 2024; 20km Individual - Men 2015, 2016, 2017, 2019, 2020, 2021, 2023, 2024; 4 x 6km Relay - Mixed 2020, 2023, 2024; 4 x 7.5km Relay - Mixed 2021; Single Mixed Relay - Mixed 2019, 2020, 2021; 2 x 6 + 2 x 7.5km Relay - Mixed 2016, 2017, 2019
  • Top finish: 5th – 2024 (4 x 7.5km Relay - Men)

bio.education: University of Notre Dame, '09 philosophy
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Graduated with a degree in philosophy in 2009 from the University of Notre Dame
  • Nickname is Avatar
  • Author of Avatar's Guide to Beach Volleyball
Career Highlights
  • Has two silver medals on the FIVB World Tour, most recently in 2017 with John Hyden
  • Prior to his beach volleyball debut in 2010, played three seasons (2005-07) in the minor league after being signed by the Arizona Diamondbacks
  • Was a baseball pitcher at Notre Dame from 2003-05, being named an All-American in 2004
  • ---
  • DID YOU KNOW?
  • Paired with Billy Allen in 2018. Competed in seven AVP events, finishing second in Hermosa and third in New York and San Francisco.
  • Played in five FIVB events with Allen. They finished tied for ninth in The Hague and Doha, Qatar. The pair also finished ninth in Yangzhou, China as part of the 2019 season
  • In 2017, had his most successful international season of his career; partnered with Olympian John Hyden for one silver, one fourth and three fifth-place finishes; on the domestic AVP Tour, won one and earned third twice
  • Tied a career best finish of silver on the FIVB World Tour, in 2017 with John Hyden in Poland
  • Has played internationally with Olympians Todd Rogers, Nick Lucena, and John Hyden
  • Has played on the FIVB World Tour since 2013
  • Between 2013 and 2016 on NORCECA, has one silver and one bronze, medaling in two of two tournaments
  • Made his international debut in 2013 at NORCECA Cayman Islands, finishing second with Todd Rogers
  • On the AVP Tour from 2010-16, has two titles, seven second-place finishes and eight third place finishes; named the domestic tour's Best Offensive Player in 2015
  • Made his professional beach debut in 2010 at AVP Huntington Beach
  • In 2011, played with three partners and finished 17th three times
  • Was a pitcher for the Notre Dame baseball team, named second team All America
More

bio.education: Wayzata High School United States Air Force Academy
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Played football, basketball, and baseball growing up in Minnesota
  • Started his handball career at the U.S. Air Force Academy after attending a walk-on tryout for the football team and getting encouraged to transfer his skillset to the sport of handball
  • Graduated in 2015 and his first assignment on active duty was Los Angeles AFB where he continued training as a member of the USA Beach and Indoor National teams
  • Was selected for the Air Force World Class Athlete Program (WCAP) in 2018, which allowed him to train full time for the U.S. handball team
  • Played for the German club, TSV Bayer Dormagen during the 2018-2019 season
  • Is playing for Abanca Ademar León in the Spanish Asobal (first division) league and in the EHF Cup during the 2019-2020 season
  • Hobbies include spending time with friends, coaching basketball, surfing, snowboarding, and activities with his church
  • Is the oldest of his siblings
  • Enjoys spending time with his family in Minnesota on the lake during the holidays and the summer.

bio.education: University of South Florida
bio.biography:
# 41 
Height: 5'5"
Position: Outfield
B/T: L/R
Hometown: Guilford, Conn.
School: South Florida
Graduation: 2018


EXPERIENCE
2016 Women's Elite Team Member
- World Cup of Softball participant
 - Hit .462 (6-for-13) with three runs scored

2015 Junior Women's National Team Member
- World Cup of Softball participant
 - Hit .500 (2-for-4) with one RBI and one run scored
- WBSC Junior Women's World Championship Gold Medalist
 - Hit .167 (1-for-6) with two runs scored

AT SOUTH FLORIDA
2016 NFCA All-Region Second Team Mideast
2016 American Athletic Conference Second Team

GETTING TO KNOW DONOVAN
Parents: Lauri and Kevin Donovan
Siblings: Amber and Blake
Pets: A pug named Sam
Dream job: To be an FBI agend
Favorite sport besides softball: Gymnastics (I stopped at the age of 12 to focus on softball) and volleyball
What it means to represent the United States: I have always had a fascination for the American flag and its colors. I have had one hanging on the wall of my bedroom as a decoration for many years. It has new meaning now as it reminds me to work towards my goals on a daily basis so I can represent my country and play for the Junior Woman's National Team. It will be the highest honor to represent my country in the sport that I have worked so hard at and love.
What she loves about softball: The competitiveness and speed as well as the ability the game has to change at any moment.
Who has been the biggest supporter of your career: My mom and dad and grandparents for always bending over backwards to take me to all the endless games and practices.
Why she chose to attend South Florida: I chose the University of South Florida for its beautiful campus, perfect softball weather and it was a location that was all new to me and I wanted to try something different!

TRIPLE PLAY:
Three people I'd love to have dinner with: Nadia Comaneci and Abraham Lincoln,
Three things I love doing: Painting, exploring and traveling
Three TV characters I wish were real so I could hang out with them: Nathan Scott from One Tree Hill
Three things I must have when I leave the house: My wallet, phone and chapstick
Three favorite movies: She's the Man, Horrible Bosses and The Last Song
Three pet peeves: People chewing with their mouth open and people complaining

ONE LAST THING
Last person I had dinner with: My sister Amber
The last gift I bought: A fuzzy, soft blanket
Last splurge: A dozen homemade donuts at the Florida Strawberry Festival
Last thing I misplaced: My car keys!
Last joke I laughed at: Anything by Jeff Dunham
Career Highlights
  • World Cup, Oklahoma City, 2016 (Team - Women)
  • Junior World Championship, Oklahoma City, 2015 (Team - Women)
  • World Cup, Irvine, 2015 (Team - Women)
